assumptions: No prior Docker account configuration or successful use exists.
The developer has the browser and command-line tools listed by the selected path.

developer_goal: Docker Hub exchanges a username and PAT for a JWT, then returns the authenticated user's repository list.

first_success.outcome: Docker Hub exchanges a username and PAT for a JWT, then returns the authenticated user's repository list.
first_success.observable_signal: The repository-list request returns successful JSON for the Docker Hub namespace.

route_selection.surface: Docker Hub PAT + Hub API JWT auth + list repositories (cURL)
route_selection.rule: Prefer the official hosted quickstart and cURL when documented; stop at the first observable successful happy-path result.
route_selection.selected: Docker Hub PAT + Hub API JWT auth + list repositories (cURL)

candidates.name: Docker Hub PAT + Hub API JWT auth + list repositories (cURL)
candidates.reason: It is the specified official path to the first-success boundary.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to Docker Hub.
required_path.observable_result: Docker Hub account settings are accessible.

required_path.action: Create a personal access token and copy it securely.
required_path.observable_result: A Docker Hub PAT is available.

required_path.action: Use cURL to exchange the Docker ID and PAT for a Docker Hub API JWT as documented.
required_path.observable_result: The authentication response contains a JWT.

required_path.action: Send the documented authenticated request to list repositories for the Docker Hub namespace.
Confirm the list request succeeded and inspect the repositories array, including an empty list for a new namespace.
required_path.observable_result: A valid repository-list response is visible.

prerequisites.description: A Docker account with access to Docker Hub.
A Docker Hub personal access token with suitable permissions.
A terminal with cURL and a JSON-handling method for the returned JWT.

external_gates.description: The PAT value is only available for secure use after creation and must carry the required permissions.

platform_outcomes.description: Docker Hub exchanges a username and PAT for a JWT, then returns the authenticated user's repository list.

excluded.reason: This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.
This occurs after the selected first-success boundary.

sources.title: Personal access tokens
evidence_supported: Docker Hub exchanges a username and PAT for a JWT, then returns the authenticated user's repository list.
The repository-list request returns successful JSON for the Docker Hub namespace.

sources.title: Export repositories from Docker Hub
evidence_supported: Docker Hub exchanges a username and PAT for a JWT, then returns the authenticated user's repository list.
The repository-list request returns successful JSON for the Docker Hub namespace.

uncertainties.question: Does fresh-account UI wording differ from the documented Authenticate and list Docker Hub repositories path?
uncertainties.impact: A human should judge minor navigation differences without moving the first-success boundary.
